title: Airbnb (ABNB) Valuation Check After Recent Share Price Gains And Conflicting Fair Value Signals
detail:
Airbnb (ABNB) Valuation Check After Recent Share Price Gains And Conflicting Fair Value Signals
Simply Wall St
March 18, 2026 3 min read
ABNB

Track your investments for FREE with Simply Wall St, the portfolio command center trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

Airbnb stock snapshot and recent performance

Airbnb (ABNB) continues to attract attention after recent trading, with the share price at US$131.95 and mixed returns across different periods, including a 2.8% gain over the past day and 8.7% over the past month.

See our latest analysis for Airbnb.

For context, Airbnb’s recent move to US$131.95 follows a 1 month share price return of 8.7%. The year to date share price return is slightly negative and the 5 year total shareholder return is also negative, suggesting momentum has cooled after earlier gains.

If this kind of price action has you looking around the market, it could be a good time to broaden your search with 20 top founder-led companies

With Airbnb shares 9.9% below the average analyst price target and an indicated 48.3% discount to one intrinsic value estimate, you have to ask: is the market underestimating the business, or already pricing in the road ahead?

Most Popular Narrative: 10.1% Overvalued

The narrative fair value for Airbnb of $119.83 sits below the last close at $131.95, so the story currently prices in a premium to that estimate.

The way people move around the world has changed. It is not only about holidays anymore. Now it is also remote work, slow travel, weekend getaways, or even trying life in a new city. Airbnb is actually responding to that, and doing it better than most.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what kind of travel mix, margins and future profit multiple support this valuation gap? The narrative leans heavily on global uptake and a business model built for flexible living.

Result: Fair Value of $119.83 (OVERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, the story could change quickly if regulatory clampdowns remove more listings, or if growth outside the US slows and weakens the case for premium pricing.

Find out about the key risks to this Airbnb narrative.

Another way to look at Airbnb’s value

The user narrative pegs fair value at $119.83, which puts Airbnb at 10.1% above the $131.95 share price. Our DCF model offers a different view, with a future cash flow value of $255.06, suggesting Airbnb may be trading at a significant discount. Which perspective do you think the market will align with over time?
